title: Implement PROP-069 — narrative-cite recalibration (Stage 1 targeting filter + Stage 2 CITATION_RE hardening + threshold recalibration to ≤15%); observational-only changes; do NOT extend to curmudgeon citation discipline (deferred to follow-up)
background:
PROP-069 (authored by tinker 2026-05-31T10:14Z in response to DIRECTIVE-006) recalibrates PROP-014 Mechanism 3's narrative-cite audit in three independent ways: (1) Stage 1 targeting filter drops daily-report.pipeline_status and daily-report.recommended_actions[].action surfaces entirely (purely operational/imperative by design), applies claim-shape paragraph filter to tinker.report.findings[].description, keeps curmudgeon.reviews.* unchanged (claim-dense by design). (2) Stage 2 CITATION_RE hardening: path-start-only matching to suppress ~42% of file-missing that are prose-with-trailing-filename mis-captures, plus a well-known-bare-filename auto-resolution table to recover another ~23% of file-missing. (3) Threshold recalibration from 0% to ≤15% claim-uncited; new citation-resolve-rate metric targeted at ≥85%; flat caps of ≤20 on bogus-anchor and file-missing.

Projected impact based on PROP-069's 3,445-paragraph sample evaluation: 3,668 claim-uncited → ~1,640 (55% reduction). The remaining 1,640 is the genuine claim-backing gap — 545/640 = 85% of curmudgeon review files have zero citations anywhere. PROP-069 reveals rather than fixes this; a follow-up BEHAVIORAL PROP for curmudgeon citation discipline is the real next step but is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this directive (deferred to a separate authoring directive after PROP-069 lands and the projected reduction materializes in actual audit runs).

PROP-069 has zero open_questions_for_operator — clean approval path. No design clarification needed before implementation. The 3 recalibration moves are independent and can ship together in one commit.

what_changed_vs_prop_014_amendment_001.stage_1_targeting_filter: Previously: every paragraph >1 sentence across all audited surfaces. New: surface-specific filters — daily-report.pipeline_status EXCLUDED, daily-report.recommended_actions[].action EXCLUDED, tinker.report.findings[].description filtered by claim-shape, curmudgeon.reviews.* unchanged.
what_changed_vs_prop_014_amendment_001.stage_2_citation_re_hardening: Previously: CITATION_RE matched any path-like pattern anywhere in the paragraph. New: path-start-only matching (anchor at paragraph/sentence boundary) PLUS well-known-bare-filename auto-resolution table (e.g., 'open-issues.json' → 'monitor/decisions/open-issues.json').
what_changed_vs_prop_014_amendment_001.threshold_recalibration: Previously: 0% uncited threshold per amendment-001 Q3. New: ≤15% claim-uncited (allows for legitimate claim-shaped paragraphs that lack obvious citation targets, e.g., novel curmudgeon arguments citing logical inference rather than source files). New citation-resolve-rate metric ≥85% (415 citations × 85% = ≥353 resolving citations). Flat caps ≤20 on bogus-anchor and file-missing categories.
what_changed_vs_prop_014_amendment_001.soft_complaint_format_update: Previously: 'narrative-cite uncited=N bogus=N'. New: 'narrative-cite claim-uncited=N file-missing=N bogus-anchor=N resolve-rate=X%'. Tinker's soft-complaints grep should be updated in the same commit to recognize the new format.

requested_work: Step 1 — Read PROP-069 in full (especially the proposed_implementation, idempotency_contract, and verification_path sections) to refresh on the exact patches needed. Verify the per-surface filter logic and the CITATION_RE hardening regex against current audit-narrative-citations.js code; flag any drift between PROP-069's described patches and the actual current state of the script (PROP-069 was authored ~20 min ago so drift should be zero).

Step 2 — Modify monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js to apply the three recalibration changes: (a) Stage 1 targeting filter per PROP-069 section §4.1, (b) Stage 2 CITATION_RE hardening per §4.2, (c) threshold recalibration per §4.3 (acceptance gates and report-output fields). Preserve all existing edge-case handling (empty prose, code blocks, markdown headers/lists, single-sentence paragraphs).

Step 3 — Modify monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3.6c (the section that constructs the soft-complaint line in AGENT_NOTES; check around line 1003 per existing code pointer) to use the new format: 'narrative-cite claim-uncited=N file-missing=N bogus-anchor=N resolve-rate=X%' instead of the old 'narrative-cite uncited=N bogus=N'. Make sure tinker's soft-complaints grep pattern in tinker.md (or wherever it lives) still matches the new format — extend grep regex if needed.

Step 4 — Run the modified audit-narrative-citations.js once against the current workspace (in a fresh clone, --dry-run mode if supported else just inspect the output file without committing) to verify the projected 55% reduction approximately materializes. Capture the result in a one-shot file: monitor/integrity/prop-069-deployment-validation-<ISO>.json with fields {projected_uncited: 1640, actual_uncited: <measured>, delta_pct: <(measured-projected)/projected>}. If delta_pct is >25% off projection, pause and surface to operator before deploying.

Step 5 — Deploy: commit + push the modified audit-narrative-citations.js + workspace-sync.md + grep updates + the deployment-validation sentinel in one batch. Manually copy workspace-sync.md to FUSE if PROP-066's Step 4c sync hasn't propagated yet (verify via canary sentinel timestamps).

Step 6 — Post-deployment canary: 24h observation window. The next workspace-sync cycle should produce a soft-complaint with the new format. Tinker Mode 2 quick-check should grep for 'claim-uncited=' to confirm new format is live. If old format ('uncited=' without 'claim-' prefix) still appears 1h+ after deployment, that's a regression — investigate.

Step 7 — Lifecycle: mark this directive completed with prop_id_implemented='PROP-069'. Flip PROP-069.json status='implemented' with implementation_commit recorded. Add a sidecar note in the proposal indicating that the 1,614 curmudgeon-uncited remainder is the next priority for a behavioral PROP — this is the audit trail for the deferred follow-up.
non_goals: Do NOT IMPLEMENT the curmudgeon citation discipline behavioral PROP in this run — operator deferred that to a separate follow-up directive (likely D31-009 or later, after PROP-069's projected reduction materializes in actual audit runs).
Do NOT touch PROP-066 / PROP-068 deployments — both are still in their 24h+7d canary windows; observe but don't perturb.

Do NOT backfill citations across the existing 1,640 claim-uncited paragraphs. PROP-069 just changes audit targeting and thresholds; the backfill (if any) is part of the curmudgeon discipline follow-up.
Do NOT change PROP-014 Mech 1, Mech 2, or the unimplemented Stage 3 (LLM-as-judge semantic match) — only Mech 3 Stages 1+2 thresholds and targeting are in scope.

Do NOT add a new audit surface (e.g., decider daily-report narration, social drafts, predictions.md). The current 3 surfaces (daily-reports + curmudgeon reviews + tinker findings) are the established scope; expanding is out of scope for this PROP.

context_pointers.workspace_sync_soft_complaint: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3.6c (~line 1003 per existing code pointer; verify in Step 1)
context_pointers.prop_014: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-014-agent-state-coupling.json + monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-014-amendment-001.json (PROP-069 is a refinement, NOT a supersession — PROP-014.json gets an amendment_ref note pointing to PROP-069 per the PROP-014 amendment pattern)
context_pointers.latest_audit_report_pre_deployment_basel: monitor/integrity/narrative-cite-audit-2026-05-31T0207Z.json (totals: 4039 paragraphs, 3668 uncited (91%), 415 citations, 264 resolve, 80 bogus-anchor, 155 file-missing). Use as comparison baseline for Step 4's deployment validation.

expected_output: Commit on origin/main containing: monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js (Stage 1+2 + threshold patches), monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (Step 3.6c format update + any grep extension in tinker.md), monitor/integrity/prop-069-deployment-validation-<ISO>.json (Step 4 sentinel), monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-069-*.json (status flipped to 'implemented' + implementation_commit recorded + sidecar note about deferred curmudgeon follow-up). Plus this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-069'.
estimated_effort: ~30 min Opus per tinker's own estimate. Script modification is the bulk (~20 min); workspace-sync.md + tinker.md grep update is mechanical (~5 min); deployment validation + commit is ~5 min.
priority_note: MEDIUM — same priority as PROP-069 authoring. Pure log-noise/signal-quality fix (no operational harm; soft-complaint is observational). The value: (a) tinker's daily reports stop carrying false-positive 'this is fine' noise, (b) the 1,614 curmudgeon-uncited remainder becomes the actionable signal for the next follow-up. This is the prerequisite for the curmudgeon discipline behavioral PROP to have meaningful data to work against.
implementation_summary: 3 changes landed in monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js (v2.0.0): (1) Stage 1 surface filter dropped daily-report.pipeline_status + daily-report.recommended_actions[].action; tinker findings filtered by severity>=moderate AND Stage 1D claim-shape gate. (2) Stage 2 CITATION_RE hardened from [^)]+ to [./\w-]+; WELL_KNOWN_PATHS table added for bare-filename auto-resolution. (3) Threshold recalibrated: claim-uncited <=15%, citation-resolve >=85%, bogus-anchor/file-missing flat caps of 20 each. New metrics emitted: claim_uncited_rate_pct, citation_resolve_rate_pct, citations_auto_resolved. monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3.6c updated to parse new keys with 14-day legacy fallback. Deployment validation against current workspace: claim-uncited 3668->1680 (54% reduction, projection was 55% — delta_pct +2.4% well within ±25% tolerance). bogus-anchor 80->17 (pass cap 20). file-missing 155->10 (pass cap 20). auto-resolved=6. Citation-resolve 54.2% still RED vs 85% target — expected and actionable; the deferred curmudgeon-citation-discipline behavioral PROP is the next step (out of scope for this directive).
